---
css: styles.css
author: gp
date: 2026-07-10
lang: en
subtitle: Why India Built One of the World\'s Largest Software
  Industries but Comparatively Little Foundational Software
title: Renting the Floor
---

#==============================================================================

#==============================================================================

#==============================================================================

#==============================================================================

<style>
body{line-height:1.55;}
.paragraph{max-width:72ch; text-align:justify; margin-top:0.8em; margin-bottom:0.8em;}
.thesis{background:#eef6ff; border-left:6px solid #2f5597; padding:1.2em; margin:1.5em 0;}
.thesis-title{font-weight:bold; font-size:1.1em; margin-bottom:0.7em;}
.summary{background:#fafafa; border-left:6px solid #666; padding:1.2em; margin:1.5em 0;}
.summary-title{font-weight:bold; font-size:1.1em; margin-bottom:0.7em;}
.notes{background:#fffdf4; border-left:6px solid #b8860b; padding:1.2em; margin:1.5em 0;}
.notes-title{font-weight:bold; font-size:1.1em; margin-bottom:0.7em;}
.story{background:#f7fbff; border-left:6px solid steelblue; padding:1.2em; margin:1.5em 0;}
.story-title{font-weight:bold; font-size:1.05em; margin-bottom:0.6em;}
.observation{background:#f9f9f9; border-left:6px solid #888; padding:1em; margin:1.5em 0;}
.definition{background:#eefaf0; border-left:6px solid #3d8b40; padding:1em; margin:1.5em 0;}
table{font-size:0.95em;}
</style>

# Core Thesis

<div class="thesis">
<div class="thesis-title">
Core Thesis
</div>
<div class="paragraph">
Applications are rented. Foundations are owned.

Operating systems, databases, distributed storage, messaging systems, language runtimes and compilers form 
the foundation upon which modern software is built.

India built one of the world's largest software industries but has only a limited presence in globally adopted
foundational software. This essay argues that this imbalance is strategically significant and that India must 
increasingly own, rather than rent, the software floor.
</div>
</div>

# Executive Summary

<div class="summary">
<div class="summary-title">
Executive Summary
</div>
<ol>
<li>India has one of the world's largest software industries.</li>
<li>Its presence in globally adopted foundational software is comparatively small.</li>
<li>Foundational software enables other software and compounds value across the software ecosystem.</li>
<li>This essay compares India with representative global and Chinese projects.</li>
<li>It studies how successful foundational software originates and how it evolves.</li>
<li>It distinguishes a project's origin from its eventual outcome.</li>
<li>It proposes that engineering constraints create foundational software, while institutional custody determines whether it survives.</li>
<li>The resulting framework is applied to India and China.</li>
<li>The framework makes predictions that can be tested against future projects.</li>
</ol>
</div>

# Notes and Assumptions

<div class="notes">
<div class="notes-title">
Notes and Assumptions
</div>
<ul>
<li>Every factual statement is independently verified.</li>
<li>References are cited at the point of use.</li>
<li>Project classifications represent the author's taxonomy unless otherwise stated.</li>
<li>Figures and tables are original unless explicitly attributed.</li>
<li>Market values are point-in-time observations used only for comparison.</li>
</ul>
</div>

# The Paradox

India\'s software inputs predict a mature foundational ecosystem. The
observed output does not match.

```{dot}
//| label: fig-india-software-paradox
//| fig-cap: "India's software inputs and the missing foundational output."
digraph G {
    graph [rankdir=TB, splines=ortho];

    {
        rank=same;

        rev [
            shape=plaintext,
            label=<<B>US$283 bn/yr</B><SUP>{revenue}</SUP><BR/>IT Revenue>
        ];

        dev_pool [
            shape=plaintext,
            label=<<B>21.9 mn</B><SUP>{total_devs}</SUP><BR/>GitHub Developers>
        ];

        new_dev [
            shape=plaintext,
            label=<<B>5.2 mn/yr</B><SUP>{new_yrly_devs}</SUP><BR/>New Developers>
        ];

        oss_contri [
            shape=plaintext,
            label=<<B>#1</B><SUP>{oss_contri_rank}</SUP><BR/>Public OSS Contributors>
        ];
    }

    expect [
        shape=box,
        label="Expected\nTechnological\nOutput"
    ];

    stack [
        shape=record,
        label="{Expected Foundational Software|{Operating \nSystems|DB|Distributed \nStorage|Message \nBrokers|Language \nRuntimes|Compilers|Cryptography|Consensus|Build \nSystems}}"
    ];

    reality [
        shape=diamond,
        label="Observed Reality ???\nFew Globally Adopted Projects"
    ];

    rev -> expect;
    dev_pool -> expect;
    new_dev -> expect;
    oss_contri -> expect;
    expect -> stack;
    stack -> reality [color="#b22222"];
}
```

<a id="fn-revenue"></a>
